Identification.LongTitle: An Act regarding the right to know when products contain toxic substances

Provision.Text: This enactment prohibits the sale, importation and advertisement of any product that contains a toxic substance or produces a toxic substance when used, unless that product has a label warning of the potential exposure to the toxic substance affixed on one or more surfaces of its packaging.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Toxic Substances Warning Label Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: food芒聙聜has the same meaning as in section 2 of the Food and Drugs Act. (aliment)
product芒聙聜means any good, including food and agricultural products, that can be produced, bought or sold, and that has a physical identity. (produit)
toxic substance芒聙聜means a substance in a gaseous, liquid or solid state listed in the Schedule. (substance toxique)

Subsection.Text: It is prohibited for any person to sell, import or advertise any product that contains a toxic substance or produces a toxic substance when used, unless that product has a warning label affixed on one or more surfaces of its packaging.
The warning label required under subsection (1) must be printed in clearly legible and indelible characters and must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a list of every toxic substance that the product contains;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the risks related to each toxic substance produced as a result of using the product;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a hazard symbol indicating the nature of the hazard of each toxic substance; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an indication of the product芒聙聶s origin, including the name and address of its manufacturer or supplier.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, on the recommendation of the Minister of Health, amend the Schedule to add or delete a substance.
The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister of Health, make regulations for carrying into effect the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the labelling of the products described in subsection 3(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the importation of products manufactured outside Canada in order to ensure compliance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: requiring persons who sell products to maintain the books and records that the Minister of Health considers necessary for the proper enforcement and administration of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: requiring manufacturers of any product to submit test portions of any batch of their product; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: providing for the sampling, testing, inspection and analysis of products as well as for the manner and conditions of sampling, testing and analysis, and designating laboratories to perform testing and analysis.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes any provision of this Act or the regulations as that provision relates to a product other than food is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding $5,000; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine not exceeding $10,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months, or to both.

Subsection.Text: Every person who contravenes any provision of this Act or the regulations as that provision relates to food, is guilty of an offence and liable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on summary conviction, to a fine not exceeding $50,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months, or to both; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on conviction on indictment, to a fine not exceeding $250,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years, or to both.

Subsection.Text: Due diligence is a defence in a prosecution for an offence under subsections (1) and (2).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Criminal liability of officers, etc., of corporations
Subsection.Text: If a corporation commits an offence under this Act, any officer, director, agent or mandatary of the corporation who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and is liable on conviction to the punishment provided for the offence, whether or not the corporation has been prosecuted or convicted.

Section.MarginalNote: Products received or in transit before Act in force
Section.Text: No person is to be found guilty of an offence under this Act in relation to the sale, importation or advertising of a product if the person establishes that the product in relation to which the offence was committed was received by, or was in transit to, the person from a supplier before the day on which this Act comes into force.
